recoveryTheme: AI approvals move quickly, but the recovery path still depends on too few named fallback owners once policy and security collide.
nextMove: Name a backup approval owner and test AI recovery under policy-review load.

recoveryTheme: Identity can recover, but audit and security reviews still stretch the restore window longer than the board packet suggests.
nextMove: Cut one duplicate review and pre-assign the recovery owner for audit exceptions.

recoveryTheme: Revenue packets reopen because the restore path lacks enough redundancy when finance and commercial signoffs drift.
nextMove: Add a backup signoff route and test revenue restore end to end.

recoveryTheme: FinTech scope looks credible, but the restore path cannot absorb much more diligence load before fallback quality collapses.
nextMove: Add load buffers and a parallel restore route before the next diligence surge.

recoveryTheme: Procurement remains the cleanest decision path and already behaves like the recovery reference model for the rest of the estate.
nextMove: Reuse the procurement recovery packet in one constrained adjacent lane.

recoveryTheme: Regulated control lanes still depend on too few durable restore paths instead of a stable fallback packet.
nextMove: Add one redundant regulated restore path and test it under exception load.
